title: No systemd-analyze output using sudo
body:
### Issue Description

`podman-system-generator` generates no output when `systemd-analyze` is executed using sudo.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a quadlet that contains some sort of error. (see below) `/etc/containers/systemd/traefik.container`
2. Verify systemd generators using sudo. `sudo systemd-analyze --generators verify traefik.service`

```
[Container]
Image=docker.io/library/traefik
Network=false.network
```

### Describe the results you received

```
$ sudo systemd-analyze --generators verify traefik.service
/usr/lib/systemd/system-generators/systemd-ssh-generator failed with exit status 1.
/usr/lib/systemd/system-generators/podman-system-generator failed with exit status 1.
Unit traefik.service not found.
```

### Describe the results you expected

```diff
 $ sudo systemd-analyze --generators verify traefik.service
+quadlet-generator[12090]: converting "traefik.container": requested Quadlet unit false.network was not found
 /usr/lib/systemd/system-generators/systemd-ssh-generator failed with exit status 1.
 /usr/lib/systemd/system-generators/podman-system-generator failed with exit status 1.
 Unit traefik.service not found.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

The expected output happens when executing `systemd-analyze` without sudo, and also when executing `podman-system-generator -dryrun` directly, sudo or no.

The `systemd-ssh-generator` error appears to be a bug in a recent Debian update. I had the same behaviour sans that error in a previous version of Debian.

comments.body: if the bug is in debian, i'm not sure what we will do about it here.  i'm ok leaving it, but just saying ...
I don't know that it's an issue with Debian. If you're referring to my last paragraph, that was just about `systemd-ssh-generator` being included in the output. I don't believe it's related.

as root we seem to log directly to /dev/kmsg

https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/blob/408f6e74b1edab4e6e761c50228dda18fc6aa633/cmd/quadlet/main.go#L44-L46

As rootless we have no permissions and fall back to stderr
https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/blob/408f6e74b1edab4e6e761c50228dda18fc6aa633/cmd/quadlet/main.go#L73-L76

It is unclear to me if that is actually true if we must write to kmsg, does systemd not forward the logs from stderr to the journal from all generators?

cc @alexlarsson @ygalblum

I've looked into it myself and also asked the LLM to help me. In summary:
1. The reason it works without `sudo` is that writing to `/dev/kmsg` fails (due to permissions) so quadlet fallbacks to using STDERR
2. I tried not writing to `/dev/kmsg` at all. This fixed the issue here. But, writing to STDERR did not show in the journal log when running `systemctl daemon-reload`
3. AI Explanation (which sounds reasonable to me):
  Systemd generators run in a very early, restricted context — no journal, no syslog, no dbus. The [systemd generator guidelines](https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/systemd.generator.html) explicitly say to log via /dev/kmsg. During daemon-reload, pid1 does not forward generator stdout/stderr to journald (see [systemd#15638](https://github.com/systemd/systemd/issues/15638)); kmsg is the reliable path into the journal.
That is why switching to stderr-only fixes systemd-analyze but breaks journalctl after daemon-reload.
4.  The LLM's recommendation was to print to both

I don't see a problem with it. WDYT?

> Since [syslog(3)](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man3/syslog.3.html) is not available (see above), log messages have to be written to /dev/kmsg instead.

Well in that sense the docs seems wrong for users generators as we cannot write there as rootless user?
looking into systemd sources I found
https://github.com/systemd/systemd/blob/aeaea4e62f01bca423087ff07bb25ac375cc8ee2/src/shared/generator.c#L1056-L1069

In general I do not get how the stderr of the rootless quadlet ends up in the journal than when systemd claims it does not capture the output for generators?


As for writing both kmsg and stderr as root I think it makes sense

Yeah, that comment is really only about the system generator, and only the run at early boot (not daemon-reload),. but that one runs before the journal is even started I think, so kmsg is the best way to get any info out of it.
Don't trust me over other sources though, its been a while since i looked at this in particular.

> Yeah, that comment is really only about the system generator, and only the run at early boot (not daemon-reload),. but that one runs before the journal is even started I think, so kmsg is the best way to get any info out of it.

I used `daemon-reload` to check when I looked into it. For root services, nothing was printed to the journal when I disabled using kmsg. My guess (and I did not look into the code) is that systemctl just assumes the generators will still write to kmsg and does the wiring on its own (to correspond with what we're doing in the generator)
